name: Alvin Alcorn
abstract: Alvin Elmore Alcorn (September 7, 1912 – July 10, 2003) was an American jazz trumpeter.

interviews.title: Alvin Alcorn and Eddie Marrero Interview, 1979-12-04

about_quotes.speaker: Narvin Kimball
about_quotes.evidence: And Alvin Alcorn playing trumpet. So we organized a group and called them the Four Tones. We played for two, three years together.

about_quotes.speaker: Dalton Richards
about_quotes.evidence: Howard Davis, and Alvin Alcorn, George Williams, the drummer, and myself, we played a little job... Alvin Alcorn on trumpet... we went to St. Francisville... and we played.

about_quotes.speaker: Henry "Pickle" Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: We had Alvin Alcorn on trumpet... And we had myself, I was on drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Steinau
about_quotes.evidence: I remember also I think although there was another audition involved too so I might get them mixed up uh Alvin Alcorn and Brother uh Brother Cornbread uh Tom what's his first name Joseph Thomas right and uh Eddie Pearson and uh Eddie Pearson and uh I can identify them more I suspect looking at the p

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Alvin Alcorn in a story about a night at Rue's Tavern; the interviewee responds with general agreement ('Yeah, that's what I'm fixing to say') but does not claim personal interaction or knowledge of Alcorn.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Fairconnetue
about_quotes.evidence: George McCollum left the band when his wife was going to have a kid and then he was sent for Alvin Alcorn. Alvin Alcorn, that's right. ... Alvin Alcorn. That was the trumpet player who replaced George McCollum.
